search_results.title: Forecasting the future of artificial intelligence with machine learning-based link prediction
search_results.snippet: AI techniques are being used to predict the future research directions of AI itself, using link prediction in exponentially growing knowledge graphs. Atom-level prediction of future links is hard but tractable with link prediction.

search_results.title: AI Is Learning to Predict the Future—And Beating Humans at It
search_results.snippet: A forecasting bot placed in the top 10 in a forecasting competition for the first time. Predicting hard things about the future is becoming more tractable with LLM ensembles.

search_results.title: Can Machine Learning Really Predict The Future?
search_results.snippet: It is difficult to predict future events with certainty. Predictions are influenced by human behavior, hard to model and atomic-level uncertainty propagates.

search_results.title: Addressing the data bottleneck in medical deep learning models
search_results.snippet: The data bottleneck refers to the difficulty in obtaining an adequate number of cases and quality data. The data-bottleneck problem is widespread across the field of machine learning.

search_results.title: SC-Block: Supervised Contrastive Blocking within Entity Resolution Pipelines
search_results.snippet: Supervised contrastive blocking improves data resolution by learning what counts as a relevant pair. Relevant data must be sampled with the right blocker at the atomic record level.

search_results.title: The New Bottlenecks of ML Training: A Storage Perspective | SIGARCH
search_results.snippet: Machine learning training moves the bottleneck to the data ingestion phase, rather than the actual learning phase. ML training datasets far exceed the DRAM capacity.

sub_claims.text: The predicted future state contains a mechanism that removes the named blocker

search_results.title: Prediction method of predicting a future state of a system
search_results.snippet: A method for embedding time series data within a reconstructed phase space to predict at least one future state of a system based on the rate of separation of trajectories.

search_results.title: Decoding predicted future states from the brain's physics engine - PMC
search_results.snippet: The physics network carries abstract information about predicted future contact events and contains a generative model of the physical world conducting forward simulations, serving as the brain's physics engine.

search_results.title: Statistical prediction of the future impairs episodic encoding of the present | PNAS
search_results.snippet: The brain prioritizes prediction over encoding when faced with the conflict between remembering specific past experiences and preparing for future situations where prediction mechanism removes uncertainty.

sub_claims.text: the prediction is therefore a market signal for the resolution mechanism

search_results.title: Prediction Market | Mechanism Institute
search_results.snippet: Prediction markets allow participants to trade outcome shares whose value depends on the resolution of specified future events. The incentive model aggregates private information into public price signals.

search_results.title: Self-Resolving Prediction Markets for Unverifiable Outcomes
search_results.snippet: Prediction market resolution mechanisms determine how outcomes are verified and settled. Self-resolving markets work even when outcomes are not directly observable; therefore market signal aligns with resolution.

search_results.title: Beyond Polymarket and Kalshi: 5 prediction markets we are paying attention to
search_results.snippet: Prediction markets turn belief into price by asking what people are willing to stake on an outcome, producing a market-driven signal that changes more regularly and perhaps more honestly than polls.

speaker_collisions.title: Reuse your FLOPs: Scaling RL on Hard Problems by Conditioning on Very Off-Policy Prefixes (arXiv 2026)
speaker_collisions.snippet: Amrith Setlur et al. 2026. Reuse your FLOPs: scaling RL on hard problems. Addresses how RL methods for LLM reasoning waste compute on hard problems where correct on-policy traces are rare.

speaker_collisions.title: POPE: Learning to Reason on Hard Problems via Privileged On-Policy Exploration
speaker_collisions.snippet: Setlur et al. 2026. Privileged on-policy exploration for hard problems in LLM reasoning. Test-time RL training.
